description_long: This is a weathered tree stump with a rough, textured bark exterior and a hollowed, open top, indicating it has been cut or broken off above ground level. The bark displays a mix of brown, gray, and green hues, with patches of moss or lichen clinging to the surface, especially on the shadier side. The base of the stump is surrounded by exposed roots that extend outward and partially above the ground, merging into a patch of compacted earth. The trunk is slightly angled, and the cut surface at the top is dark and uneven, suggesting decay or rot within. The overall form is irregular, with the roots providing a natural, organic spread at the base. This object would be commonly found in parks, sidewalks, or urban green spaces where trees have been removed but the stumps remain.
description: A weathered, mossy tree stump with exposed roots and a hollowed, dark top, surrounded by compacted earth. The bark is rough and textured, with green and brown tones.
description_view_0: The stump is seen from a side angle, showing a rough bark surface with some moss and a few roots extending outward at the base.
description_view_1: This view highlights more exposed roots on the right side, with a prominent patch of green moss and a clearer view of the uneven ground around the stump.
description_view_2: The back of the stump is visible, showing a darker, more moss-covered bark and several thick roots spreading out from the base.
description_view_3: A side view with less visible moss, focusing on the textured bark and a few roots emerging from the base into the surrounding earth.
